title: Apply backpressure instead of unbounded buffering
description: Bound in-flight concurrency and queue depth so a slow downstream slows the producer rather than exhausting memory.
body: Use when one component produces requests faster than a downstream can serve them. Do not buffer requests in an unbounded queue or spawn unlimited concurrent calls, because a slow dependency then turns into an out-of-memory or descriptor exhaustion failure. Actions: cap concurrent in-flight calls (semaphore/pool size), bound any queue and decide a block/drop/shed policy, and propagate slowness upstream (block the producer or return a busy signal). Validation signals include memory and connection counts staying flat under a slow downstream and the producer slowing rather than crashing. Risk boundary: a buffering change that alters drop vs block semantics affects data loss; confirm the policy and escalate if it can drop committed work.
